entries.title: Lipton v BA Cityflyer [2024] UKSC 24
entries.summary: UK Supreme Court confirmed pilot illness is not an extraordinary circumstance. Carrier must demonstrate adequate crew rostering to avoid liability. Key ratio: internal crew shortages caused by illness are within carrier control.
entries.actionNote: Review all open cases with crew illness as pleaded extraordinary circumstance — liability position weakened

entries.title: CAA UK261 Evidence Standards — Updated Guidance May 2026
entries.summary: CAA published updated enforcement guidance requiring specific evidence of extraordinary circumstances. ATFM data, METAR records, and crew statements are specifically referenced as expected evidence. General assertions no longer sufficient.
entries.actionNote: Ensure evidence packs include specific ATFM references and METAR records — not general operational summaries

entries.title: Flightright v Iberia (C-188/26) — Hidden defects clarification
entries.summary: CJEU confirmed a technical defect caused by a component failure unknown to the manufacturer does not automatically qualify as extraordinary. Carrier must prove the defect could not have been discovered through reasonable maintenance inspection.
entries.actionNote: Review maintenance records for open technical cases — reasonable inspection standard applies

entries.title: AviationADR — Updated submission portal requirements Q2 2026
entries.summary: AviationADR updated its case submission portal. Evidence bundles must now be submitted as a single PDF not exceeding 20MB. Separate exhibits no longer accepted. Cases submitted without a merged bundle will be returned unprocessed.
entries.actionNote: Update evidence bundle process — merge all exhibits into single PDF before ADR submission

entries.title: Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers Act 2024 — ADR framework in force 6 April 2026
entries.summary: DMCCA 2024 ADR provisions came into force. New mandatory ADR timelines apply across EU member states. Airlines must now respond to ADR referrals within 30 days rather than 90. Non-compliance exposes carriers to enforcement action.
entries.actionNote: Update ADR deadline engine for FR/ES cases — 30-day response window now mandatory
